{"query":"Temu illegal hazardous products EU investigation specific products defective chargers toxic baby toys enforcement statement 2025","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1579,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.euronews.com/video/2026/05/28/eu-fines-chinese-e-commerce-giant-temu-200-million-for-dangerous-baby-toys-and-faulty-char","title":"Video. EU fines Temu €200m for dangerous baby toys and faulty chargers | Euronews","content":"EU fines Temu €200m for dangerous baby toys and faulty chargers"},{"url":"https://www.today.com/video/eu-fines-temu-232-million-over-illegal-and-unsafe-products-264174149685","title":"EU Fines Temu $232 Million Over Illegal and Unsafe Products","content":"The Chinese online retailer Temu was hit with a $232 million fine after a European Union investigation found it failed to protect consumers from illegal products like toxic or hazardous toys and unsafe electronics."},{"url":"https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c1k2ydn1rz8o","title":"EU fines Temu €200m for allowing sale of illegal products","content":"EU fines Temu €200m for allowing sale of illegal products
Temu has been under investigation since October 2024 over whether it has been meeting its obligations as a designated Very Large Online Platform under EU law.
The investigation involved a mystery shopping exercise carried out by an independent testing organisation, which found that a high percentage of chargers purchased through Temu failed basic electrical safety tests.
The fine is only the second imposed under the EU's Digital Services Act for content, the first being a €120m penalty against Elon Musk's X social media network last December."},{"url":"https://www.dw.com/en/temu-fine-eu-europe-unsafe-toys-noncompliant-products/a-77336877","title":"EU fines Temu €200M over unsafe toys, non-compliant products","content":"The European Union has handed Chinese-owned online retailer Temu a €200 million ($232 million) fine on Thursday for noncompliance of the bloc's Digital Services Act (DSA), a rule that compels the world's most popular digital platforms to conduct risk assessments of potential dangers to consumers.
It is the second such fine imposed under the EU's DSA on content, after Elon Musk's X platform received a €120-million fine in December.
EU regulators said that consumers on Temu are "very likely to encounter illegal items" and that the retailer "seriously underestimated how often EU consumers are likely to" see such products.
The Chinese retailer must now pay the fine and present a plan to the EU by August 28 to address the EU's concerns.
But the EU began to take a closer look at Temu since October 2024 when an investigation found in July last year that Temu had breached landmark rules over the risks of illegal products.
Virkkunen said that there was a threat that illegal products from Temu were easily available to too many EU consumers.
On Thursday, the EU said it had opened an in-depth investigation into Chinese e-commerce giant JD.com's bid for a major German electronics retail group Ceconomy."},{"url":"https://www.bostonherald.com/2026/05/28/temu-fine-unsafe-toys-electronics/","title":"Temu hit with $232 million fine over unsafe toys and electronics","content":"The European Commission said Temu failed to identify, analyze and assess the systemic risks of illegal goods for sale on the platform and the resulting harm to European consumers.
Investigators had carried out a “mystery shopping exercise” that turned up a number of “non-compliant” products, including many electronic device chargers that failed basic safety tests."}]}
